response: {"calibration_score":0.52,"overconfident_count":3,"underconfident_count":1,"well_calibrated_count":12,"biases_detected":["anchoring: assumed .env.local path and service ports from prior session state without verifying first","tool-availability assumption: used heredoc+jq pipeline without checking for encoding hazards, required multi-step recovery","recency bias: inferred dev-server liveness from pm2 list rather than direct port probe, leading to false-negative curl failures","path-existence optimism: repeatedly issued bash/grep against paths that did not exist before confirming with ls/glob"],"recommendations":["probe environment state (port liveness, file existence, env vars) in a single batched step at session start rather than lazily as failures surface","when writing multi-line jq or shell scripts via heredoc, prefer Write tool over bash heredoc to avoid shell quoting and encoding corruption","treat tool failures as calibration signals: two consecutive failures on the same resource (e.g. .env.local) should trigger an explicit existence check before a third attempt","unit -13 produced no output despite an accepted user turn — consider whether a brief acknowledgement or state-check would have been more useful than silence"]}
